steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. Sentence 1 states that Falconer explores Fernández’s sculpture. Sentence 2 states that Falconer participates in the tradition of ekphrasis. The transition must show that the act of exploring the sculpture is precisely what constitutes participating in ekphrasis — a cause-and-effect or means-and-result relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "In addition,": Signals additive information, implying sentence 2 is a separate, independent point. But sentence 2 is not a separate action — it is the consequence of sentence 1. ✗
  • "In doing so,": Means “by performing the action just described.” By exploring the sculpture, Falconer participates in ekphrasis — exactly the means-and-result link the passage requires. ✓
  • "That said,": Signals concession or contrast. No contrast exists between the two sentences; Falconer’s exploration and his participation in ekphrasis are aligned, not opposed. ✗
  • "Conversely,": Signals direct opposition. The sentences do not contradict each other, so this transition is logically wrong. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
